source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I received your letters of most bitter import about the death of the Lord Maximianus in the month of November.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And he indeed has reached the rewards he longed for, but the unhappy people of the city of Syracuse is to be commiserated as not having been counted worthy to have such a pastor long.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Accordingly let thy Love take anxious heed that such a one may be chosen for ordination in the same church as may not seem to obtain undeservedly the same place of rule after the lord Maximianus.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And indeed I believe that the majority would choose the presbyter Trajan, who, as is said, is of a good disposition, but, as I suspect, not fit for ruling in that place.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Yet, if a better cannot be found, and if there are no charges against him, he may be condescended to under stress of very great necessity.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if my wishes are asked with regard to this election, I inform thee privately of what I do wish: for no one in this same church appears to me so worthy after the lord Maximianus as John the archdeacon of the church of Catana.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, if his election can be brought about, I believe that he will be found an exceedingly fit person.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But he too must first be enquired about by thee privately as to any charges against him that may stand in the way.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: If he should be found free from any, he may be rightly chosen.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Should this be done, our brother and fellow-bishop Leo will also have to give him leave to go, that he may be found free to be ordained.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: These things, then, I have taken care to intimate to thy Love; and it will now be thy concern to look round thee on all sides carefully, and arrange what is pleasing to God.
